name: Rebirth
description: A morally or emotionally diminished character undergoes renewal through crisis, restoring integrity or humanity.

nodes.label: Fallen State
nodes.definition: The protagonist exists in a condition of moral, emotional, or spiritual corruption. They may be powerful but are diminished in humanity — cold, selfish, cruel, or numb.
entry_conditions: The audience sees the character's corruption or emptiness
exit_conditions: An external event or figure disrupts the character's insulated life
typical_variants: Wealthy miser who exploits others
Powerful figure who has lost empathy
Ordinary person frozen by grief or cynicism
failure_modes: Character is so repulsive the audience has no investment in their renewal
Fallen state is told rather than shown through behavior

nodes.label: Warning or Catalyst
nodes.definition: An event, encounter, or message signals that the character's current path leads to destruction. This is the first crack in the character's self-justification.
entry_conditions: An outside force intrudes on the character's world
exit_conditions: The character is unsettled but not yet changed
typical_variants: A stranger or innocent challenges the character
A prophecy or vision of consequences
Loss of something the character didn't realize they valued
failure_modes: Warning is too subtle to register
signals_in_text: An encounter that leaves the character uneasy
A mirror held up to their behavior

nodes.label: Resistance and Denial
nodes.definition: The character dismisses or fights the warning, doubling down on their current behavior. Their defenses are tested but hold — for now.
entry_conditions: The character's identity is invested in the fallen state
exit_conditions: A deeper crisis overwhelms the character's ability to deny
typical_variants: Lashing out at the messenger
Rationalizing harmful behavior
Attempting to reassert control over others
failure_modes: Resistance is perfunctory — character gives in too quickly
No escalation between warning and deeper crisis
signals_in_text: Anger at those who challenge them

nodes.label: Ordeal of Truth
nodes.definition: A shattering experience forces the character to see the full consequences of their fallen state. Denial is no longer possible. This is the moral and emotional bottom.
entry_conditions: An undeniable confrontation with truth occurs
exit_conditions: The character accepts what they have become and feels the weight of it
typical_variants: Vision of their impact on others
Loss of the last person who cared
Confrontation with their own death or irrelevance
Witnessing the suffering they caused
failure_modes: Ordeal is too gentle — character doesn't hit bottom
Ordeal is external only — no internal reckoning

nodes.label: Turning Point
nodes.definition: The character makes a conscious choice to change. This is not yet redemption — it is the decision to pursue redemption, often at personal cost.
entry_conditions: The ordeal has broken through denial
The character sees a path to change, however difficult
exit_conditions: The character takes a concrete action that demonstrates the new commitment
typical_variants: Public confession or apology
Quiet resolve to make amends
Giving up the source of their corrupt power
Reaching out to someone they wronged
failure_modes: Turning point is purely internal with no visible action
Change is instantaneous with no struggle
signals_in_text: A decision made aloud or in action
First genuine act of generosity or humility

nodes.label: Atonement
nodes.definition: The character acts to repair the damage they caused, demonstrating that the change is real. Atonement is costly and may not be fully accepted by those harmed.
exit_conditions: Enough genuine repair has occurred that the character's change is credible
typical_variants: Material restitution
Selfless act that risks the character's remaining status
Reconciliation with a specific wronged person
Public reversal of a harmful policy or practice
failure_modes: Atonement is too easy — no one resists or doubts
Atonement is performative with no real cost
Character expects forgiveness as a reward

nodes.label: Renewal
nodes.definition: The character emerges renewed — not restored to a prior state, but transformed into someone more whole. The world around them reflects the change.
entry_conditions: The character has paid the cost
exit_conditions: A new equilibrium is established that integrates the character's past and their transformation
typical_variants: Joyful reintegration into community
Quiet peace and acceptance
Death with dignity after the change is made
New relationships built on genuine connection
failure_modes: Renewal erases all consequences — everything is magically fine
Renewal is purely sentimental with no structural change in the character's life

edges.label: First Crack Appears
edges.preconditions: Fallen state is fully established; an outside force intrudes
edges.effects_on_stakes: The character's insulated world is no longer perfectly sealed
edges.effects_on_character: First moment of discomfort or doubt — quickly suppressed
edges.common_alternatives: Warning comes as a supernatural event; warning comes from a child or innocent
edges.anti_patterns: Warning is so weak the character has no reason to notice

edges.label: Digs In
edges.preconditions: Warning has been received; the character's ego resists
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each act of resistance worsens the character's situation and relationships
edges.effects_on_character: Defenses harden but become more brittle
edges.common_alternatives: Resistance is active (punishing the messenger) or passive (ignoring the warning)
edges.anti_patterns: Character resists for so long the story stalls

edges.label: Truth Breaks Through
edges.preconditions: Resistance is at maximum; an undeniable experience occurs
edges.effects_on_stakes: The character faces total loss — of self-image, relationships, or meaning
edges.effects_on_character: Denial collapses; the character sees themselves clearly
edges.common_alternatives: Ordeal is triggered by the character's own actions backfiring; ordeal is imposed by an external event
edges.anti_patterns: Ordeal is a gentle realization rather than a shattering experience

edges.label: Chooses Change
edges.preconditions: The ordeal has broken denial; the character sees a choice
edges.effects_on_stakes: The character risks losing everything they have left to pursue change
edges.effects_on_character: Agency is reclaimed — the character acts rather than reacts
edges.common_alternatives: Choice is dramatic and public; choice is quiet and private
edges.anti_patterns: Change happens automatically after the ordeal with no deliberate choice

edges.label: Makes Amends
edges.preconditions: The turning point is made; the character must now act on it
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each act of atonement costs the character something real
edges.effects_on_character: The character is tested on whether the change will hold under resistance
edges.common_alternatives: Atonement is a single grand gesture; atonement is a series of small acts
edges.anti_patterns: Atonement is accepted immediately by everyone — no skepticism or pushback

edges.label: Renewal Achieved
edges.preconditions: Atonement has been credibly demonstrated; the cost is paid
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes dissolve into a new and more genuine stability
edges.effects_on_character: The character is fundamentally different from who they were at the start
edges.common_alternatives: Renewal is celebrated by community; renewal is private and quiet; character dies having achieved it
edges.anti_patterns: Renewal erases all consequences; the fallen state is forgotten rather than integrated
